_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 9-35R Morphine (Revised 12/10/2025, MDHHS Reviewed 2026). Base Michigan template.
protocol_reference: Michigan Medication Protocol Section 9-35R: Morphine — MDHHS Approved 1/5/2026, Reviewed 2026
precautions: Monitor respiratory rate, SpO2, and mental status continuously
Minimum 10 minutes between IV/IO repeat doses
IM route: single dose — no repeat
Elderly patients: reduce dose; increased sensitivity to opioids
expected_effects: Significant analgesia within 5-10 minutes IV, 20-30 minutes IM
side_effects: Hypotension (especially with rapid IV push or hypovolemia)
notes: Maximum single dose: 0.1 mg/kg; maximum 5 mg per dose
IV/IO: may repeat up to 3 times total; maximum cumulative 20 mg; minimum 10 minutes between doses
IM: single dose only — no repeat
Contraindicated <18 months — use fentanyl for infant pain management
Pediatric >18 months: same dosing as adult (0.1 mg/kg max 5 mg)

dosing.dose: 0.1 mg/kg; maximum 5 mg per dose; may repeat up to 3 times (max total 20 mg); minimum 10 minutes between doses
dosing.notes: Slow IV push; monitor respiratory status; have naloxone available

dosing.dose: 0.1 mg/kg; maximum 5 mg; single dose — NO repeat
0.1 mg/kg; maximum 5 mg per dose; same dosing structure as adult
dosing.notes: Same dosing and restrictions as adult; NOT for patients ≤18 months
